## [00:00 - 03:03]

Alright guys, so let's have a look at how to put together the copy for what we call your direct response marketing. Now direct response is where you are going out to a cold audience and attracting them into your offer or your funnel. So it is basically the copy that you're going to be using for your Facebook ads. Now typically with this industry we're working with what we call long form copy. So it's generally a couple paragraphs long at the very least. Now for those of you that are not copywriters by nature this might be a little bit of an intimidating thought. How do you pull together a long form piece of copy for your ad creative? This is the process that we use and it's fairly painless. Now it does take time to establish your personas and hopefully you did go through that step and you did dedicate the time that you needed to properly flesh this out. So for example here is, I've basically cut this down a little bit just so I could give you guys an example but my personas worksheet, I've got the wants, the pain points and then the proof as well as the story. I've got two personas in here existing coaches and consultants, new coaches and consultants. And I've listed out their wants. So for example existing coaches, they want to automate their sales processes, they want to serve as many people as possible without it requiring more time on their end. They want to gain maybe more time freedom from their business, etc. etc. This can take a couple hours to map out especially if you need to do it with your client or if you need to dig into some of the customer responses that they have had in their database or go on Amazon and search and dig a little bit better. You really want to put a lot of effort into mapping this out and ideally you're copying and pasting in your ideal customer avatars very same words. So you can check out that hack around how to pull really good direct response copy using your existing customers and that basically just copies and pastes what they want in their exact same lingo. So once I have this, basically the framework is pretty simple. I'm just going to weave my pain points, my wants, my proof, my stories together. And I can weave them in any kind of combination. The one thing that does stay the same normally with this kind of copy is that the offer will always be at the end. So I will, for example, start off with pointing out some of the pain points or discussing, talking about some of the pain points, what they want. I'll go into the story, maybe my background and then I'll say, hey, if this is something that you're interested in, come and check out this training where I'm going to be, etc.

## [06:04 - 08:38]

So by just tying it back to some of the copy that I said over here, work for you. Okay, so I might go and edit this a couple times. I'll also get someone else on my team to review it for spelling and grammar mistakes because it can be super easy to overlook that when you're writing your own copy. But basically this is the structure. So I mean, and it doesn't even have, there's a lot more variations to this guys. But all you're doing is once you've established this and you've really flushed this out properly, you basically can create, you know, 30, 40, 50, hundreds of different pieces of direct response marketing that is essentially all saying the same thing, but in different ways. And so every single week, for example, you could go and create new ads without really needing to do anything else other than just tapping into the personas worksheet that you've flushed out and restructuring it in a different way. So this is the framework. You can just pick one pain point and talk about that where you can try to put all of them in bullet points. You can just take one one and focus on that. You can just focus on one part of the story and then pull in all the wants, but you're stitching it all together to create the copy, which ultimately speaks to that persona that you are focusing on. So you can see here, this sheet here is the most important thing you can do. Once you flush this out properly, this is going to be very, very easy to do. Now when you're in your third round of rapid fire testing, you may also want to add in a different column to your personas worksheet called plan offer where you now you now know what the offer of the lead magnet is going to be. You can also leave this into your copy. So for example, here I'm going straight for the kill saying like join me. So I'm obviously using this copy in my first round of rapid fire testing.
